old_claim.note: Town Market Squares provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct Town Market Squares to stone_paved_courtyards edge remains because the node is rescoped to Pre-Pottery Neolithic ritual enclosures, not urban market paving.

source_shape.source_locator: UNESCO describes Gobekli Tepe as Pre-Pottery Neolithic monumental enclosures probably used for public rituals by hunter-gatherer groups, with no market-square context.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source anchors the node in ritual megalithic enclosures at Gobekli Tepe before urban market-square infrastructure.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because the cited site is ritual enclosure architecture rather than a market or exchange space, town market squares are not a source-backed prerequisite.

ontology_before: The node depended on town market squares, conflating ritual or architectural enclosure surfaces with later urban market infrastructure.
ontology_after: The node is scoped to Gobekli Tepe megalithic enclosures and retains only masonry as the source-backed architectural enabler.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: removed the town_market_squares prerequisite. Preserved: the node remains an Ancient source-checked built-environment technology.
would_reject_if: The node is renamed back to urban market paving or formal market courtyards.
A source shows the cited Gobekli Tepe enclosures functioned as town market squares.
A separate urban_market_paving node is introduced and intentionally depends on town_market_squares.
short_reason: Gobekli Tepe ritual enclosures are not urban market-square infrastructure.
